title: PowerShell -replace operator is global by default
description: Using `$content -replace $pattern, $replacement` replaces ALL occurrences of $pattern, not just the first. When injecting content into structural files (XHTML, JSON, config), this can multiply the injection at every match site, ballooning file size and breaking syntax.
detection: Scan .ps1/.psm1 for `-replace` used in file-write contexts (Set-Content, Out-File) where the replacement contains structural markers (begin/end comments, JS/JSON braces). Flag if the source pattern is expected to match exactly once.
remediation: For deterministic single-occurrence replacement use [regex]::new($pattern).Replace($content, $replacement, 1) with explicit count=1. Always grep-assert single occurrence after sync (pattern from SIN-PATTERN-033).
examples:
# Bad — duplicates injection at every anchor match
$content = $content -replace '/\* MARK \*/', $newBlock
